revision.text:
[[ja:赤猪岩神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Akaiiwa Shrine |image = File:Akaiiwa-Jinja 02.jpg |address = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|qid=Q917024|lt=Nishihaku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|qid=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}}寺内232 |deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|de|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|lt=Sashikuni Wakahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=刺国若比売命|lt_de=Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Inadahime|de|Inadahime|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}} |sacred_object = |status = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|de|UnRanksierter Schrein|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |foundation = Unknown |main_hall = |alternative_name = |sited = |annual_festival = |rituals = |ISO = JP-31 |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 21 |latitude_sec = 55.0 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 20 |longitude_sec = 57.3 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tottori |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.36527777777778|N|133.34925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} '''Akaiiwa Shrine''' (あかいいわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|qid=Q917024|lt=Nishihaku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}}, and {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|qid=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Unranked Shrine|de|UnRanksierter Schrein|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. ==Deities== ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} ; Secondary Deities * {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|de|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|lt=Sashikuni Wakahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=刺国若比売命|lt_de=Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (Sashikuni Wakahime) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Inadahime|de|Inadahime|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}} (Inadahime-no-Mikoto) ==History== It is located at the site that served as the setting for the myth of the {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|Hare of Inaba|qid=Q2330988|lt=White Hare of Inaba|1=Hare of Inaba}} as recounted in the upper volume of 『{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}』[http://www.kojiki.org/seikai/jokan/jokan.htm {{ill|National Treasure: Original Kojiki of Shinpuji|de|National Treasure: Original Kojiki of Shinpuji|ja|国宝 真福寺本古事記原文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure: Original Kojiki of Shinpuji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure: Original Kojiki of Shinpuji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] and in Volume 4, the Chronicle of Local Deities of 『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyūjibonki|1=Kujiki}}』[http://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/sendaikuji/sendaikuji04.htm {{ill|Volume IV, Chronicle of Local Deities|de|Volume IV, Chronicle of Local Deities|ja|先代舊事本紀卷第四 地祇本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volume IV, Chronicle of Local Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volume IV, Chronicle of Local Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}], specifically in the {{ill|Continuation of the Legend|de|Fortsetzung der Legende|ja|続きの説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Continuation of the Legend|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Continuation of the Legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Ōkuninushi’s myth. The founding date of its predecessor, Akai Ino Shrine, is unknown, but the land on which this shrine stands is said to be the setting of that tale. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|1917|en|1917|qid=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}}), Kuukiyu Shrine, built at the current location, and Akai Ino Shrine, formerly situated atop {{ill|Mount Yōgaisan|de|Mount Yōgaisan|ja|要害山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yōgaisan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yōgaisan|1=Mount Yōgaisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630038}} in Nanbu Town, were enshrined together in Taishō 9 ({{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}}) to form the present Akaiiwa Shrine. ==Myth of Ōkuninushi== The synopsis of the myth is as follows: The many elder brother deities of Ōnamuchi (later known as Ōkuninushi) resented him for marrying {{ill|Yagami-hime|de|Yagami-hime|ja|八上比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}, as foretold by the White Hare, and conspired to kill him. They led Ōnamuchi to the foot of Mount Temayama in {{ill|Hōki|de|Hōki|ja|伯耆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|1=Q11381487|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381487}}, and instructed him, "Drive the rare red boar from the mountaintop and capture it below; if you do not, you shall be killed." As Ōnamuchi waited at the foot of the mountain, his elder brother deities dropped brightly red, fire-heated rocks from the mountaintop toward him. Mistaking the rolling, glowing rocks for a boar, his body immediately clung to the scorched, charred rock and was incinerated. Upon learning this, his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|de|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, lamented deeply and ascended to {{ill|Takamagahara|en|Takamagahara|qid=Q1141974|lt=Takamagahara|1=Takamagahara}} to seek aid from the divine progenitor, {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}}. In response, two goddesses, {{ill|Red-shell and Clam Goddesses|de|Red-shell and Clam Goddesses|ja|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|lt=Goddesses of the Red Shell and Clam|lt_ja=赤貝の神・𧏛貝比売（キサガヒヒメ）と蛤の神・蛤貝比売（ウムギヒメ）|lt_de=Red-shell and Clam Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red-shell and Clam Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red-shell and Clam Goddesses|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11296639}}, were dispatched to earth. The goddess represented by 𧏛The character 𧏛 is written as {虫 beneath 討}. used a seashell to pry Ōnamuchi’s body from the rock, while the goddess represented by the clam applied a medicinal paste—prepared from her breast milk and water drawn from a clear water well—to his body. Consequently, Ōnamuchi’s severe burns were miraculously healed, and he was restored to his former radiant appearance, reviving him. ==Shrine Grounds== The rock, which is said to have incinerated and killed Ōnamuchi, is enshrined within the shrine groundsThe rock is believed to be buried deep within the earth; what is visible is a large stone, layered with multiple coverings, and surrounded by fences and shimenawa.. Additionally, a spring—reputedly the clear water well from which the clam goddess drew water to prepare the medicinal paste—exists approximately a 30‐minute walk from Akaiiwa Shrine. ==Local Information== ; Address * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|qid=Q917024|lt=Nishihaku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|qid=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}}寺内232 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}, {{ill|Yonago Station|en|Yonago Station|qid=Q1139558|lt=Yonago Station|1=Yonago Station}}; approximately 25 minutes by car after disembarking. ==Notes== ===Sources=== {{Footnote Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://www.town.nanbu.tottori.jp/akaiiwa/ Nanbu Town Office Akaiiwa Shrine] {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Shinto Cross|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤猪岩神社|version=92372841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:赤猪岩神社]] [[de:Akaiiwa-Schrein]] {{Commons category|Akaiiwa-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11635823}} [[Category:鳥取県の旧無格社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]]
